title: acaena hirsutula
intro: Acaena hirsutula, also known as hairy cat's ear or hairy variegated pewterwort, is a perennial plant native to New Zealand and Australia, characterized by its hairy leaves and yellow or white flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Acaena hirsutula include its potential to treat wounds, reduce inflammation, and combat infections, making it a useful herb for skin and respiratory issues. Acaena hirsutula can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, allowing for various herbal preparations to be used for medicinal purposes.
